rationales.security: She keeps rehearsing for parent-teacher conferences and says she would rather have "every answer ready" than be caught unprepared, showing a strong need for predictability and protection from downside.
rationales.conformity: Her worry about "stumble[ing] in front of someone asking about their child" shows careful self-monitoring to meet others' expectations and avoid social disapproval.

rationales.stimulation: She resists the move because "my stomach keeps tightening" and focuses on why Berlin would be hard, rejecting the uncertainty and novelty her partner finds appealing.
rationales.security: She emphasizes the rent-controlled flat, savings, nearby parents, and wanting "a guarantee," which shows a strong preference for stability over a risky new opportunity.
She sets up grocery delivery, meal prep, and recurring appointment reminders, actively creating a more stable and protected situation for her family during recovery.
rationales.benevolence: She organizes practical support for her mother and Tyler and says "I won't miss another one," showing sustained care for the needs of close family members.

rationales.hedonism: She describes "dark circles," missed appointments, eating meals standing up, and advises Tyler to "take a day off without feeling guilty," showing a pattern of neglecting rest and present wellbeing.
rationales.benevolence: She tells Tyler she will "help him look at programs" and wants him to keep "being there for your people," which centers support and care for someone close to her.

rationales.achievement: He takes pride in having built the team "from twelve people to forty-two" and in owning the processes and KPI framework, showing strong investment in competence and performance.
rationales.power: The meeting slight and possible merger matter because of status and control; "those are mine" shows attachment to authority over his division and how it is positioned.
rationales.security: He checks LinkedIn so he will not be "caught off guard," treating career uncertainty as a threat and preparing defensively for stability.

rationales.hedonism: He and Nadia would rather have gone to "that new seafood place" and he has already made the reservation, explicitly carving out a pleasurable experience for them.
rationales.conformity: He says there is "something calming about fitting into the role everyone expects" and he does not want to be "the source of that look" of family disappointment.
rationales.tradition: He spends the day supporting his parents' 40th anniversary, enduring the formal toasts and family expectations to honor an important family ritual.
rationales.benevolence: He spends most of the afternoon helping his parents coordinate the event and rearranges everything to make the celebration go smoothly for them.

rationales.achievement: Even after the holiday, his focus shifts quickly to work: he wants the Q1 planning meeting to go well and wants the Oman route data "properly credited this time," showing concern with performance and recognition.
rationales.benevolence: He and Nadia finally talk openly about the fertility issue and jointly decide to pause treatment, showing care and responsiveness within the relationship.

rationales.security: He checks the failing water heater, calls the plumber, worries about the replacement cost, and keeps checking it before bed, showing strong concern with household safety and financial stability.

rationales.stimulation: He rejects Héctor's suggestion because he would rather not "start over" or "gamble on something looking better from the outside," showing active avoidance of change and uncertainty.
rationales.security: He prefers the union protections and says "I stay and I wait for more information," explicitly choosing the safer, more predictable option over a potentially better but less secure move.
